additional_constraints: At least one course must be from the Faculty of Mathematics and at least one course must be from the Faculty of Engineering. Exceptions may be granted by the option co-ordinator if logistically infeasible.
Five of the courses must be considered elective (not required courses) in the student's major. A course a student could choose to graduate without is considered elective.
Special topics courses, independent project courses, and capstone project courses may sometimes be appropriate with approval of the AI option co-ordinator and the course instructor.
To use BME588 towards this Option, the topic title must be 'Introductory Mechanics of Biomedical and Biological Materials'.
KIN420 may count only towards one requirement.
The two-term project topic must be in the area of biomechanics and students are encouraged to have their projects supervised or co-supervised by a faculty member outside of their home department.
One course must be considered 'extra' to your degree. Contact the Option co-ordinator for information on requirements and restrictions.

declaration_requirements: Before declaring this academic plan, see invalid credential combinations.

Students may not declare this Option until they have completed a List 1 course and a List 2 course. Students must have a minimum average of 75.0% calculated across these two courses in order to declare this Option.
additional_constraints: Five of the courses must be considered as electives in the student's major academic plan.
Students who take CS136 or CS146 are also required to enrol in CS136L. However, passing CS136L is not a requirement for the Option and the course may be coded as NRNA.
Exceptions to the courses listed here may be approved by the Option co-ordinator.

declaration_requirements: Before declaring this academic plan, see invalid credential combinations.

Students may not declare this Option until they have completed both an introductory programming course (List 1) and a data structures and algorithms course (List 2). Students must have a minimum average of 75.0% calculated across these two courses in order to declare this Option.
additional_constraints: Three of the courses must be considered as electives in the student's major academic plan.
Students who take CS136 or CS146 are also required to enrol in CS136L. However, passing CS136L is not a requirement for the Option and the course may be coded as NRNA.
Exceptions to the courses listed here may be approved by the Option co-ordinator.

additional_constraints: At least three courses must be MSE-labelled courses.
A maximum of one course from outside the approved list may be counted towards the Option, subject to approval of the option co-ordinator.
Students may take both MSE211 and MSE311; each course may only be used towards one requirement.

Students may not declare this Option until they have completed a List 1 course and a List 2 course. Students must have a minimum average of 75.0% calculated across these two courses in order to declare this Option.
